hero_subtitle: Geospatial Engineer | Climate Researcher | ETH Zurich
branding_text: Combining environmental science with tech to build real-world climate solutions through geospatial AI and Earth observation.
audience_note: This portfolio is crafted for researchers, recruiters, and curious minds who want to explore how space tech meets climate challenges.
geospatial_vision_text: Passionate graduate student specializing in climate science and environmental data analysis, with hands-on experience in satellite-based flood detection and land use dynamics. Skilled in leveraging machine learning and geospatial technologies to address climate resilience and sustainability.
career_objective_text: To apply my expertise in geospatial analysis and remote sensing to develop innovative solutions for climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction, while contributing to interdisciplinary research at the intersection of technology and environmental science.
research_focus_text: Currently pursuing my Master's in Geomatics Engineering at ETH Zurich, I'm driven by research that informs practical, tech-enabled climate solutions. My work spans from GNSS-based precipitation prediction to flood risk modeling using advanced geospatial tools and satellite data.
geospatial_explorer_subtitle: Click here to explore what this exciting field is all about!
satellite_data_text: Like a doctor uses X-rays to see inside the body, we use satellite images to see Earth's health from space. We process data from various satellite sensors (optical, radar, LiDAR) to monitor environmental changes, track deforestation, measure sea level rise, and detect urban expansion.
mapping_analysis_text: Think of Google Maps, but with superpowers to predict floods, track deforestation, and monitor climate change. We create intelligent maps that help understand complex environmental patterns, analyze spatial relationships, and make data-driven decisions for sustainable development.
ai_solutions_text: Similar to facial recognition, but for Earth's features - identifying floods, droughts, and urban growth patterns. We train computers to automatically detect environmental changes from satellite data using deep learning models like CNNs and transformers, enabling real-time monitoring at global scales.
why_it_matters_text: Geospatial engineering helps solve some of the world's most pressing challenges:
disaster_prediction: Predicting natural disasters before they happen
climate_monitoring: Monitoring climate change impacts in real-time
urban_planning: Improving urban planning for sustainable cities
real_world_text: When a flood occurs, our systems can automatically detect the affected areas from satellite images within hours, helping emergency responders save lives and allocate resources efficiently.
eth_specialization: Specialization in Remote Sensing and Climate Science
eth_project: Semester Project: "GNSS-ZWD based Precipitation Prediction using Machine Learning"
eth_coursework: Coursework: Space Geodesy, Cartography, Environment Monitoring, Photogrammetry
vit_specialization: Specialized in Geospatial Technology and Water Resources
vit_thesis: Thesis: "Flood Risk Assessment using SWAT Model and Remote Sensing"
vit_achievement: CGPA: 9.33/10.0, 2nd rank in department out of 150 students
isro_task1: Developed a U-Net model for flood detection using Sentinel-1 SAR data, achieving 92% accuracy
isro_task2: Processed and analyzed satellite imagery for disaster management applications
isro_task3: Implemented automated pipelines for large-scale geospatial data processing
concordia_task1: Designed a geotextile filtration system, reducing lake water nutrients by 50%
concordia_task2: Conducted water quality analysis using remote sensing data
concordia_task3: Presented findings at international environmental engineering conference
ta_task1: Mentored ~40 students in GIS tasks like LULC classification and flood mapping
ta_task2: Developed course materials for geospatial analysis using open-source tools
ta_task3: Conducted workshops on satellite data processing and applications
gnss_description: Machine learning model for rainfall prediction using GNSS signal delay and ERA5 datasets with satellite-derived atmospheric data.
flood_description: SWAT model with NSE: 0.84 and R²: 0.90 for flood risk analysis using satellite imagery and hydrological modeling.
groundwater_description: AHP analysis using satellite data and GIS to identify 89% medium to high potential groundwater zones.
planttraits_project: Better Maps of Plant Functional Traits
planttraits_description: Deep learning framework for global 1 km² plant trait mapping using a shared backbone, task-specific heads, and multi-source Earth observation data.
precipitation_abstract: Analyzed precipitation impacts on water resources in the Cauvery Basin using satellite data and hydrological models.
geological_journal: Journal of the Geological Society of India • 2024
hec_ras_abstract: Studied river cross-section width for flood detection using HEC-RAS and GIS with satellite-derived DEMs.
chilika_abstract: Investigated LULC impacts on Chilika Lake's ecosystem services using multi-temporal satellite imagery.
wetland_abstract: Presented research on filtration systems for mesotrophic lakes at environmental engineering conference.
mapsam2_paper: MapSAM2: Adapting SAM2 for Automatic Segmentation of Historical Map Images and Time Series
mapsam2_journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing • 2026
mapsam2_abstract: Adapts SAM2 for automatic segmentation of historical map images and time series, enabling scalable extraction of cartographic information.
mitacs_description: Awarded prestigious MITACS scholarship for international research internship at Concordia University, Canada, selected among top candidates nationwide.
hackathon_description: 1st place in national competition for "Implementable Local Action Plan for Road Safety". Proposal was considered for implementation by IIT Madras.
rank_description: Secured 2nd rank in Civil Engineering department with CGPA of 9.33/10.0, recognized for academic excellence among 150 students.
scholarship_description: Awarded university merit scholarship for three consecutive years recognizing consistent academic performance in top 5% of class.
aicte_description: Selected among 10 students nationwide for educational tour to ATAL Tunnel, sponsored by Government of India to study advanced construction techniques.
blog_description: I'm currently developing a blog to share insights on geospatial technologies, climate research, and the intersection of AI with Earth observation.
writing_description: I occasionally write technical articles about geospatial technologies, climate science, and engineering solutions.
guest_speaker_desc: Invited to share my academic journey and research experiences with incoming civil engineering students during orientation program.
flood_presentation_desc: Presented research on flood-prone zone identification in Bihar using HEC-RAS modeling and satellite data analysis.
connect_text: Passionate about geospatial technologies and climate solutions. Open to research collaborations and impactful projects.
choose_mode_text: Use Professional mode for recruiters, or Curious mode to include educational sections.
ra_task1: Designed data pipelines for large-scale experimental atmospheric modeling.
ra_task2: Built reusable Python workflows for large-scale geospatial time-series processing and spatial feature extraction.
ra_task3: Developed machine learning models for spatiotemporal prediction using GNSS and ERA5 meteorological datasets.
ra_task4: Presented these results at the Swiss Geoscience Meeting conference in Bern (Dec 2025).
cartography_abstract: Explores how cartographic design choices affect interpretation quality in geospatial decision-making workflows.
footer_text: Dev Shree Saini | Geospatial Engineer & Climate Researcher
